guide_review_response.summary: Iteration 2 of Stage 2 Executor Agent (EA) instance 3 comprehension-gate review. All 8 corrections from iteration 1 are fully addressed. Pickup verification (V0a-V0g) re-confirmed PASS. The 12 Python entrypoints and 7 PowerShell (PS1) scripts are now explicitly enumerated, with on-disk Get-ChildItem evidence confirming la_merge_approve.ps1 lives at tools/scheduled-tasks/ (not tools/fleet_ops/) and that escalation_notify.ps1 + test_async_post_gate.ps1 exist at the cited paths. Parameter-name standardization on -BlarAIRoot is applied uniformly across all 7 PS1. Code-Risk-4 (vikunja_mcp/server.py inclusion) and Code-Risk-6 (extended grep patterns) commitments are concrete and testable. Risk labels are descriptive (compound labels that include reference prefixes such as "R12-PS1-coverage-watch" or "w3-blarai-pid-preflight-watch" are acceptable because the anchor field disambiguates them from the source IDs they reference -- they are not bare-numeric relabelings of the kind iteration 1 flagged). Rollback explicitly acknowledged. The w3 preflight stop-and-report discipline is committed in both the ack list and item 2.8's on_drift block. The legacy_consistency_rule for tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py correctly surfaces the dual-touch hazard across items 2.4 and 2.6. The single open question (critical_notify.ps1) is healthy escalation rather than silent decision-making and is answered in the answer_to_ea_open_question block below. Twenty-three acknowledgments restated verbatim (7 Guide-#4 + 9 critical_carryovers + 3 plus_guide_3 + 4 plus_iteration_1_acks) -- ack discipline is solid. EA may proceed to item 2.4 work after emitting the two small acknowledgments below; no further Guide re-review required for those acks.

answer_to_ea_open_question.question_recap: tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 surfaced via the expanded 3-directory PS1 audit scope. Not enumerated in 2.4.v2 additional_tools or G-1/G-4. Should it be added to canonical 7 (becoming 8), or surfaced as defer-decision in closing handoff?
answer_to_ea_open_question.answer:
Default disposition: NOT added to canonical 7. critical_notify.ps1 was already audited in WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1, which classified it as "NO ACTION" because its only BlarAI path reference is a comment-example (line 11), not runtime code. It was intentionally excluded from 2.4.v2 additional_tools and from G-1/G-4 for that reason. The expanded 3-directory audit scope correctly surfaces it as a side effect, but the prior audit answer stands.
Inspection rule at item 2.5 (mandatory; not optional): 1. Physically inspect tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 to confirm Appendix A.1 still holds: no runtime $RepoRoot or hardcoded BlarAI path; only comment-example references. 2. If confirmed (expected outcome): document the no-action disposition in the closing handoff with explicit citation to WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1, and proceed with the canonical 7 unchanged. 3. If a runtime BlarAI path is discovered (unexpected; would invalidate Appendix A.1's audit): STOP and report to Lead Architect. Do NOT silently expand the canonical 7 to 8 mid-stage. The Lead Architect will decide whether to add critical_notify.ps1 to scope (with -BlarAIRoot refactor) or defer to a follow-up ticket.
Same inspection rule applies generically to any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope surfaces beyond the canonical 7: default = no-action with documented citation to Appendix A.1 (or other prior audit); runtime-path discovery = STOP and report; never silent scope expansion.

required_acknowledgments.acknowledge: I will inspect tools/fleet_observability/critical_notify.ps1 at item 2.5 and confirm the WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 audit finding (BlarAI reference in comment only, no runtime path). If confirmed: document the no-action disposition with explicit Appendix A.1 citation in the closing handoff. If a runtime BlarAI path is discovered: STOP and report to Lead Architect before any refactor or scope expansion.

The same inspection rule applies to any other PS1 the expanded 3-directory audit scope (tools/scheduled-tasks/, tools/fleet_ops/, tools/fleet_observability/) surfaces beyond the canonical 7: default = no-action with documented citation; runtime-path discovery = STOP and report. Never silent scope expansion mid-stage.

I will begin item 2.4 (Python entrypoints argparse refactor) work after emitting the two acknowledgments above in the EA chat. The 16 pre-existing binding acknowledgments + 7 Guide-#4 acknowledgments + these 2 critical_notify-watchpoint acknowledgments remain in force through Stage 4 cutover (per i1-2 dedup rule).

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization: PROCEED — you may begin item 2.4 (Python entrypoints argparse refactor) immediately after emitting the three acknowledgments above. No separate Guide re-review is required for those acknowledgments; they are for audit-trail purposes. All other work-plan items (2.4.v2 through 2.10) flow as enumerated in your iteration-2 comprehension gate. Standing reminders applicable throughout: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9; fleet stays paused (verify state.json on each substantive edit; never call state.resume_fleet); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; append-only STATUS.md edit at item 2.10. If context exhaustion threatens before item 2.9, emit a v3 mid-stage handoff XML following the v1/v2 schema (docs-only commit), then stop — no partial-commit refactor and no emergency single-commit attempts that omit pending items. On completion, emit the closing handoff including V0a-V0g + V1-V7 + per-item V8 outputs plus your decision on the legacy tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py treatment (route through new chokepoint OR fold in entirely) and your critical_notify.ps1 inspection result.
